title: Table Management on MySQL
text: Table Management on MySQL Tables are created on the fly, or by SQL scripts generated with by the Create Table utility. When you first attempt to access a table, Uniface needs to know if it exists. If the table does not exist, Uniface creates and indexes it, using the information from the application model. The table is created in the following way: The create table statement is issued. The primary key constraint is created within table creation. Indexes are created with the create index statement. Overflow tables are created on the fly when needed. For more information, see MySQL Data Packing . An overflow table contains the primary key from the base table, and the overflow information from the field that causes the overflow table. The name for an overflow table consists of the same name as the lowercase entity name with the prefix ‘o’. Note: The overflow mechanism is a technical encoding for larger amounts of data, and is not transparent to other applications. Segmented Field Support The U4.0 MQL connector supports segmented fields. By default, packing codes with the prefix S (SC, SR, SU, SW) are mapped to large object data types. This can be disabled using the connector option segmented fields: disabled . In this case, Uniface uses the overflow tables mechanism. For more information, see segmented fields . For the MQL U2.1 and U3.0 connectors, the overflow table mechanism is used to support large data fields. Limits For specific details about the MySQL Server limits for records, indexes, tables, and fields for your chosen storage engine, consult the MySQL documentation. Was this topic helpful?
